concerns.text: T014.5 (extract test set predictions) and T015 (calculate MAE on test set) are listed as sequential after Phase 2, but T011.5 (Train/Test Split) is marked as incomplete/unchecked in Phase 2. T014.5 explicitly consumes the output of T011.5. The plan implies T014.5 can run before T011.5 is finished, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule. T014.5 must be blocked until T011.5 is checked.

T021 (Wilcoxon test) and T020.1 (RF test evaluation) depend on T011.5 (Split) to provide the held-out test set. T011.5 is currently unchecked. T020.1 and T021 are listed as implementation tasks in Phase 4, but their execution is impossible without the artifact from T011.5. The dependency chain is broken; T011.5 must be a hard prerequisite for T020.1.

T030 (Cross-reference substructures) explicitly depends on T030.1 (Create Rules File). T030.1 is marked as incomplete/unchecked. T030 cannot execute without the SMARTS patterns defined in T030.1. This is a direct producer-consumer violation within the same phase.

T014 processes the 'full dataset' (train + test). T014.5 extracts the test set from T014's output. While T014 is checked, T014.5 is unchecked. The dependency is valid, but T014.5 is a critical blocking task for T015 and T021. The current ordering suggests T014.5 is optional or deferred, but it is required for the statistical test (FR-005). T014.5 must be marked as a mandatory prerequisite for T015/T021.

T050 and T051 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depend on T009 (Preprocessing) and T011.5 (Split) to target the correct held-out set. While the text notes the dependency, the [P] tag is misleading if these tasks cannot run until T011.5 is complete. The [P] tag should be removed or the dependency explicitly noted as a hard block in the task description to avoid parallel execution attempts on missing data.
